runs.interpretation: IES for nondurable consumption in an annual macro-calibration for a representative U.S. household, distinct from risk aversion.

runs.reasoningSummary: My central belief is that the annual macro-calibration IES for nondurable consumption is below 1 and plausibly around 0.5. I put substantial mass in the 0.3–0.8 range because micro and aggregate consumption evidence often suggests limited but nonzero willingness to shift nondurable consumption intertemporally, while values above 1 seem possible but not my default for this specific representative-household calibration target.
